summary: Independent QA review accepts the M2 district list implementation. The current feat/m2-client-district-list worktree satisfies the district panel read-model fields, 4000-row synthetic pressure fixture, manual virtualization, filtering/sorting/selection handoff, Playwright coverage, performance evidence, and boundary requirements. No client_engineer changes are requested.

tests_run.result: Worktree contained the task client/web/test changes, allowed orchestration state changes, writer handoff/routes, and untracked task test files; no unrelated forbidden-path implementation changes were observed before writing this QA handoff.
Only expected WorldState string assertions in tests matched; no production sim-core, Node/Electron, real-time, or RNG imports/usages were found in the task files.
Vitest passed 1 file / 4 tests, covering the synthetic fixture, filtering/sorting, virtual window, and read-model attachment.
Vitest passed 1 file / 2 tests, covering district panel fields and initial virtualized rendering.
Vite production build passed; 23 modules transformed.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism hashes matched; root Vitest passed 18 files / 85 tests; web Vitest passed 1 file / 3 tests.

Playwright Chromium passed 2 tests, including the 4000-row virtualized district list, filtering, sorting, selection handoff to panel/map, and measured timing attributes. The pre-existing Vite dynamic import warning for hello-simulation-adapter.mjs still appeared.
Prettier check, ESLint, recursive typecheck, and architecture check passed.

decisions: Accepted Playwright coverage as satisfying the task's Storybook-or-Playwright requirement.
Accepted the manual fixed-row virtualization because it keeps the UI dependency surface unchanged and tests assert rendered row count remains bounded.
Accepted the district data as synthetic pressure fixture content because naming and provenance explicitly state it is not formal historical district content.
Accepted boundary compliance because the UI consumes client-core read-model fields and task files do not import sim-core, Electron, Node fs, RNG, or real-time APIs.
risks: The implementation is still an uncommitted worktree on feat/m2-client-district-list; lead integration must preserve scope and include the untracked test/handoff files intentionally.
The web shell currently wires a synthetic district fixture rather than a live worker query projection; this remains appropriate for this pressure-fixture task but needs later integration work.

The selection timing attribute measures click handler/setter overhead rather than full React commit latency; filtering/sorting derivation timing and Playwright interaction coverage are sufficient for this task, but fuller UI profiling can be a future test_engineer follow-up if needed.
The pre-existing Vite dynamic import warning from apps/web/src/worker/hello-simulation-adapter.mjs remains visible during Playwright and was not modified by this task.
requested_action: Proceed with lead integration gates for M2-CLIENT-DISTRICT-LIST-001; no client_engineer repair is requested.
